name: aws-compliance-checker
description: Automated compliance checking against CIS, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and SOC 2 benchmarks
capabilities: Run CIS AWS Foundations compliance checks across IAM, logging, monitoring, and networking
Validate PCI-DSS requirements including network security, encryption, and access controls
Check HIPAA compliance for healthcare workloads covering access controls and encryption
Generate automated compliance reports with pass/fail status and scores
Audit security groups for overly permissive rules (0.0.0.0/0 access)
Verify CloudTrail, AWS Config, and VPC Flow Logs are properly configured
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 83 potential issues but manual review confirms all are false positives. External commands are legitimate AWS CLI invocations for compliance checks. Network patterns are standard CIDR notation (0.0.0.0/0) for security group auditing and legitimate documentation URLs. C2 keywords and weak crypto flags are triggered by normal security compliance terminology. This is a defensive security tool for AWS compliance auditing.
valueStatement: Manually auditing AWS environments against CIS, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and SOC 2 benchmarks is time-consuming and error-prone. The AWS Compliance Checker skill automates compliance validation by running AWS API checks and generating detailed compliance reports.
userTitle: Check AWS Compliance Against Industry Standards
limitations: Requires valid AWS credentials with read-only access to IAM, EC2, CloudTrail, and other services
Does not automatically remediate findings - only detects and reports
Does not cover all controls in each framework - provides representative checks
Results depend on AWS API permissions and may miss resources in unsupported regions

useCases.targetUser: Security engineers and compliance officers preparing for audits
useCases.title: Pre-Audit Compliance Validation
useCases.description: Run full compliance checks before external audits to identify and fix issues proactively.

useCases.targetUser: DevOps and platform teams managing AWS infrastructure
useCases.title: Continuous Compliance Monitoring
useCases.description: Integrate into CI/CD pipelines or scheduled jobs to maintain ongoing compliance posture.

useCases.targetUser: Cloud architects and security leaders needing comprehensive compliance views
useCases.title: Multi-Framework Compliance Reporting
useCases.description: Generate unified reports covering CIS, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and SOC 2 in a single run.

promptTemplates.title: Run CIS Benchmark Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Run CIS AWS Foundations compliance check on my AWS account
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick compliance validation against CIS benchmark

promptTemplates.title: Generate PCI-DSS Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a PCI-DSS compliance report for my AWS environment

promptTemplates.title: Check HIPAA Compliance
promptTemplates.prompt: Check HIPAA compliance for my AWS account, focusing on encryption and access controls

promptTemplates.title: Audit Security Groups
promptTemplates.prompt: Audit all security groups in my AWS account for overly permissive rules and generate a report

bestPractices: Run compliance checks regularly (weekly or monthly) to catch configuration drift early
Use AWS Organizations to run checks across all accounts in your organization
Document exceptions and remediation plans for any failed checks
Integrate with AWS Security Hub for centralized compliance dashboards
antiPatterns: Running checks only once before audits instead of continuously monitoring
Ignoring warnings about unused credentials or overly permissive access
Relying solely on automated checks without manual security reviews
Not maintaining evidence documentation for audit trails

faq.question: What AWS permissions are needed to run these checks?
faq.answer: The skill requires read-only access to IAM, EC2, CloudTrail, CloudWatch, S3, and AWS Config. Use IAM read-only policies or the SecurityAudit AWS managed policy.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically fix compliance issues?
faq.answer: No, this skill only detects and reports compliance issues. Remediation requires manual action or separate automation scripts.

faq.question: Which compliance frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: Currently supports CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and SOC 2. Each framework has representative checks covering key requirements.

faq.question: Can I run checks across multiple AWS accounts?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use AWS Organizations with cross-account roles or run the checks separately in each account and aggregate results.

faq.question: How often should compliance checks be run?
faq.answer: Best practice is to run checks at least weekly, with continuous monitoring via AWS Config Rules for production environments.

faq.question: What happens if AWS API calls fail during a check?
faq.answer: The check will report partial results with error messages indicating which checks could not complete due to permission issues or service unavailability.
